steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what point the text makes specifically about *gogogs*. The answer must be directly stated in the passage, not inferred.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'It contains a repetition of the element "go" in *gogs*.': The passage states explicitly: "the element 'go' in *gogs* gets repeated in *gogogs*." This is a direct match. ✓
  • Choice B — 'It doesn't have a clear equivalent in English.': The passage never mentions English or any comparison to English. Not stated. ✗
  • Choice C — 'It is the only word in O'odham that uses reduplication.': The passage says the opposite — "there are many examples of this type of reduplication in O'odham." ✗
  • Choice D — 'It is identical in meaning to several other words in O'odham.': *Gogogs* means "several dogs" — it is related to *gogs* ("a dog"), but the passage never claims it is identical in meaning to other O'odham words. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
